iu.text1: Hello player! Unforgettable adventures await you with IU! Here you will find a variety of technologies: from thermal to quantum, from simple improvements to technologies for transforming objects. IU is a unique mod with its concept of progress, which is unparalleled. The main rule of IU is to collect every resource, because every resource is necessary to advance progress in IU.

iu.research1.info1: §7⎜ The first interface you see is the solar system selection.
iu.research1.info2: §7⎜ By default, the §eSolar System §7is available, but others may exist.
iu.research1.info3: §7⎜ To conduct research, you must insert a research lens.
iu.research1.info4: §7⎜ Each celestial object has a level. Without the required level, viewing is unavailable.
iu.research1.info5: §7⎜ Additionally, you need to craft a §6rocket launch pad§7.
iu.research1.info6: §7⎜ Space in this mod is a development simulation.
iu.research1.info8: §7⎜ Each object must be explored. They can provide resources (items and liquids).
iu.research1.info9: §7⎜ Once a celestial object is §a100% §7explored, a colony can be established.
iu.research1.info10: §7⎜ A colony is a construction simulation. It allows large-scale resource extraction without player involvement.
iu.research1.info11: §7⎜ Some celestial objects may be unavailable for research for various reasons.
iu.research1.info12: §7⎜ Rovers can be launched at §a0% §7research, probes at §a20%§7, satellites at §a50%§7, and rockets at §a80%§7.
iu.research1.info13: §7⎜ The better the spacecraft, the more valuable the resources from expeditions.
iu.research1.info14: §7⎜ Upgrade your spacecraft to the maximum if this option is available.
iu.research1.info15: §7⎜ Under improper conditions (§ehigh temperature§7, §8pressure§7, §blow temperature§7), the spacecraft may be lost.
iu.research1.info16: §7⎜ More tips will be provided in future parts.
iu.research2.info1: §7⎜ You can view objects on the map.
iu.research2.info2: §7⎜ Use the §6mouse wheel §7to zoom in or out.
iu.research2.info3: §7⎜ You can also move the map by holding the §6left mouse button§7.
iu.research2.info4: §7⎜ On the right, you can see a list of planets available for exploration.
iu.research2.info5: §7⎜ Click on a planet to open its information window.
iu.research2.info6: §7⎜ To exit the interfaces, use the button in the top left corner.
iu.research2.info9: §7⎜ Spacecraft also need to be charged.
iu.research2.info10: §7⎜ If §8fuel §7or §benergy §7runs out, the spacecraft will be lost.
iu.research2.info11: §7⎜ Items and fluids are loaded into the rocket launch pad
iu.research2.info12: §7⎜ To do this, place §6tanks §7from this mod around it.
iu.research2.info13: §7⎜ More tips will be provided in future parts.
iu.research3.info1: §7⎜ This is the planetary research interface.
iu.research3.info2: §7⎜ In the lower right corner, satellites are displayed.
iu.research3.info3: §7⎜ Each satellite becomes available at a certain percentage of the main planet’s research progress.
iu.research3.info4: §7⎜ If the main planet is unavailable, its satellites can still be viewed.
iu.research3.info5: §7⎜ The screen displays the primary information about the planet.
iu.research3.info7: §7⎜ The list of detected resources depends on the research percentage of the planet or satellite.
iu.research3.info8: §7⎜ Press the "§eLaunch§7" button if the spacecraft is loaded onto the launch pad.
iu.research3.info9: §7⎜ On the screen, you can visually track the spacecraft's location and remaining time.
iu.research3.info10: §7⎜ If you forgot something, you can recall the spacecraft by clicking "§eRecall§7".
iu.research3.info12: §7⎜ Monitor §8fuel §7and §benergy §7levels. The enabled automation mode will automatically refuel and recharge the spacecraft.
iu.research3.info13: §7⎜ Once research reaches §a100%§7, the "§eEstablish Colony§7" button will appear.
iu.research3.info14: §7⎜ A similar interface is available for satellites with the same functionality.
iu.research3.info15: §7⎜ To create a colony you need: §athree §7"§aResidential Module I§7", §atwo §7"§aFactory I§7", §aone §7"§aOxygen Module I§7"
iu.research3.info16: §7⎜ §aOne §7"§aProtection I§7", §aone §7"§aSolar Panel I§7", everything should be in the inventory.

smeltery_info3: §7⎜ To pour liquid into the tank, §6right-click§7.
smeltery_info5: §7⎜ If more than two liquids are selected, they can be combined.

electronics_assembler.info3: §7⎜ Press the button to the left of the line.
electronics_assembler.info4: §7⎜ Three colored stripes will appear on the line: §eyellow§7, §cred§7, and §agreen§7.
electronics_assembler.info8: §7⎜ If the arrow hits the §8gray §7color, nothing happens.

soldering.info3: §7⎜ When the recipe starts, an arrow will appear.
soldering.info4: §7⎜ Two colored stripes will appear on the line: §cred §7and §agreen§7.
soldering.info5: §7⎜ Press the "§e→§7" and "§e←§7" keys to control the arrow.
soldering.info8: §7⎜ If the arrow hits the §8gray §7color, nothing happens.
soldering.info9: §7⎜ If the arrow stays on the §cred §7line for too long, the recipe will be completed instantly.
soldering.info10: §7⎜ There is a chance of losing resources if the recipe fails.

steam_machine.info1: §7⎜ This information applies to all steam machines.
steam_machine.info2: §7⎜ To operate the machine, you need §8steam§7, as well as §epressure§7 or §belectricity§7.
steam_machine.info3: §7⎜ §8Steam §7cannot be added or removed via §9cells§7 — use §esteam pipes§7, §eboilers§7, or §egenerators§7.
steam_machine.info4: §7⎜ §ePressure§7 is generated in the §epressure converter§7, which requires §8superheated steam§7.
steam_machine.info5: §7⎜ Set the pressure according to the machine's requirements.
steam_machine.info6: §7⎜ To find out the required pressure, hover over the value §a0.0§7 (if it is displayed).
steam_machine.info7: §7⎜ If the pressure in the converter is set to  «§a2§7», and the machine requires «§a1§7», it will not work.
steam_machine.info8: §7⎜ If there are no pressure requirements, set «§a0§7» in the converter to reduce consumption.
steam_machine.info9: §7⎜ Some steam machines require heating — you can use §6lava§7 or §6basalt lava§7.
charged_redstone.info: §7Drop §a4 §7units of redstone dust, §a1 §7unit of polonium dust, and §a1 §7electrum ingot into water.
iu.bee.info1: §7⎜ The bee colony operates on the principle of §6queens §7and §8drones§7.
iu.bee.info2: §7⎜ §8Drones §7are divided into gatherers, healers, defenders, and builders.
iu.bee.info3: §7⎜ Gatherers collect products from crops and accelerate their growth by §a1 §7second.
iu.bee.info4: §7⎜ Each crop can be pollinated only §aonce §7per second.
iu.bee.info5: §7⎜ For the colony to function properly, a §eprimary flower §7must be present.
iu.bee.info6: §7⎜ §eRoyal jelly §7and §ehoney §7are produced from the §epollen of the primary flower§7.
iu.bee.info7: §7⎜ Honey can also be collected from other crops.
iu.bee.info8: §7⎜ The more pollen collected, the higher the chance of mutation and obtaining additional resources.
iu.bee.info9: §7⎜ Bees can work in any biome, but their effectiveness is reduced by §a50%§7 outside their native biome.
iu.bee.info10: §7⎜ Bees can get sick and die from diseases, and have a §climited lifespan§7.
iu.bee.info11: §7⎜ §9Healer bees §7treat sick individuals, but if they are sick themselves, they cannot heal others.
iu.bee.info12: §7⎜ §cDefender bees §7protect the hive and attack incompatible bees.
iu.bee.info13: §7⎜ §eBuilder bees §7are necessary for reproduction and the creation of offspring for queens.
iu.bee.info15: §7⎜ A bee can recover without a healer if it has royal jelly.
iu.bee.info16: §7⎜ Bees feed on honey — make sure it doesn't run out!
iu.bee.info17: §7⎜ Bees can mutate due to various factors.
iu.bee.info18: §7⎜ If all §8drones §7in the colony die, the §6queen §7will also die.
iu.bee.info19: §7⎜ Bees do not work or consume food if the time of day is not suitable.
iu.bee.info20: §7⎜ Bees can help each other in difficult situations — healing, feeding, and sharing royal jelly.
iu.crop.info1: §7⎜ Each crop requires a specific type of soil and is suitable for specific biomes.
iu.crop.info2: §7⎜ To reproduce plants, use §edouble posts§7, then §6right-click §7again.
iu.crop.info3: §7⎜ Place grown crops (either the same or different) next to each other for reproduction.
iu.crop.info4: §7⎜ §8Bees §7can pollinate plants, accelerating their growth and increasing the chance of reproduction.
iu.crop.info5: §7⎜ There is a small chance that §2weeds §7may appear, and this chance depends on the type of crop.
iu.crop.info6: §7⎜ To prevent weeds from appearing, use §2pesticides§7.
iu.crop.info7: §7⎜ Growth can be accelerated with fertilizers, but after the §a40th §7application, the chance of weeds increases.
iu.crop.info8: §7⎜ Fertilizer can be applied repeatedly every §a10 §7seconds.

iu.analyzer.jei: To mine a mineral vein, use one of the following machines: analyzer, wireless mineral quarry, Simply Quarries quarry, or quantum quarry. To mine resources with the analyzer, place chests for storage next to it, install the "Quarry" module, and after the analysis is complete, click the "Mine" button. The process for the wireless mineral quarry is described in the machine's interface. The quantum quarry automatically digs under the vein. The regular quarry needs to be configured in the interface by selecting the "Vein from ore" mode, after which it will start mining.

iu.guide_quest_description.start: Welcome, player, to the beginning of this mod. This guidebook will assist and direct you in exploring the mod. The information is structured by the mod creator and is accurate, though it may vary depending on the version. In this tablet, you can view and complete quests. A "detection" quest means you must have the specified resources in your inventory. For fluid-based quests, you must have a fluid container with the required fluid. You may also have the fluid or items separately. If no items are listed, the quest can be completed immediately. This "Getting Started" tab will help you understand the basic concepts of the mod. It’s recommended to go through it before asking questions. The mod also has addons to help with progression — they are shown in the top-right of the tablet. You can also join the mod's community: suggest ideas, help newcomers, or ask questions. If you encounter bugs/crashes/dupes, report them to the developer via Discord or GitHub — links are in the top-left of the tablet. Quests are also known as tasks. Tasks can be moved freely like advancements in Minecraft. Some quests are hidden — you must complete previous ones first.
iu.guide_quest_description.energy: This mod uses a unique type of energy — Energy Flux (EF). It does not connect to machines powered by FE (Forge Energy). To link EF with other energy types, use the converter from Power Utilities (top right of the tablet). Energy is transmitted via wires. Each wire has a maximum capacity per machine (e.g., if the output is 50 EF, and two machines consume 32 and 18 EF, and the wire supports up to 32 EF, it won’t burn out, as no single channel exceeds the limit). The transformer system also works differently than in other mods. Energy is tick-based, and the max level is calculated by the formula: 8 * 4^tier. Wires can burn out, and machines can explode.
iu.guide_quest_description.vein: Vanilla ore spawn is disabled in this mod — instead, ore veins are used. A vein is a special generation area with unique characteristics. 1. You can identify a vein by surface rocks. The more types of rocks, the larger the vein. Up to 5 rocks — 1–3 stacks of ore (small), 5–10 — 3–7 stacks (medium), 10–15 — 7–15 stacks (large). When hovering over a rock, you’ll see generation percentages — this indicates ore volume. Mineral ore (always 50%) spawns only in the center and doesn't count toward the total. It can be processed in a mineral separator. You can also get ore from rocks using a ferromanganese hammer. It’s safe to dig under surface rocks — ore is guaranteed underneath. Veins typically appear between Y levels 40 and 70. Vein shapes: sphere, torus, linear, and cubic.
iu.guide_quest_description.heat: The mod adds a heating and cooling system for machines. There are two types of heating: 1. recipe-based heating, 2. heating required for a recipe. For the second type, use heaters (fluid or electric). Primitive and steam machines can be heated by placing lava beneath them. Heaters can be configured with a max temperature — this increases fluid or energy consumption. Recipe-based heating is more common in multiblocks or quarries (e.g., Simply Quarries). Cooling is done using solid-state, fluid, or electric coolers. Heating is biome-dependent: hotter biomes heat faster, colder ones cool faster. Modules can replace heaters/coolers, and special pipes are used to transfer heat, cold, or both.
iu.guide_quest_description.energies: The mod features multiple types of energy. Quantum Energy (QE) is used for quantum machines and is only generated by quantum generators or Power Utilities converters. Conversion rate: 16 EF = 1 QE. Solar Energy (SE) is generated by solar generators and depends on sun position. It can be configured via modules. Experience Energy (EE) is made from liquid experience, which can be converted using machines (like experience storage), or obtained from quarries, furnaces, or directly from the player. Radiation energy is dangerous and will be detailed later. Breaking a machine with radiation leaves contamination in the chunk — requiring cleanup. Steam and biomass energy are used similarly — to power machines. Night Energy is the inverse of solar energy. Current energy (A, Amperes) is used to charge items with pure energy. Positron energy is used in cyclotrons to create heavy elements.
iu.guide_quest_description.radiation: Radiation is one of the most dangerous mechanics in the mod. It’s usually detected in chunks using a dosimeter. It appears due to contamination from broken radiation machines or radioactive veins, often uranite and euxenite. Being in an irradiated chunk causes radiation buildup in your body. To remove it, use a radioprotector or die. Use a protective suit. Stronger radiation requires an upgraded suit. Accumulated radiation affects gameplay — players receive effects based on radiation level.
iu.guide_quest_description.volcano: Volcanoes are massive structures that only spawn in mountainous regions. Their height ranges from 50 to 120 blocks. In older versions, chests with resources could be found inside. It is very dangerous to enter without heat-resistant armor — you’ll receive poisoned air effects. Basalt can be used to obtain hydrofluoric acid. You can also mine sulfur and boron ores inside volcanoes. Some ore veins generate within volcanoes. There is also lava inside, which can be collected.
iu.guide_quest_description.pollution: Pollution is caused by machine operation. Air pollution increases every second within a 10-chunk radius. Every 5 minutes, pollution levels halve — this applies to soil as well. Pollution does not spread. At soil pollution level 2, the player gains slowness for 10 seconds, at level 3 — weakness, at level 4 — nausea. These effects may overlap. Air pollution effects: level 2 — nausea, level 3 — blindness, level 4 — gas poisoning. Use a pollution scanner to check levels. Use an air separator to clean air pollution, and soil purifier machines for soil pollution. Soil pollution also affects plant growth in the chunk.
iu.guide_quest_description.bee: Bees are one of the most important parts of progression. They have a unique mechanic. First, find a hive — they spawn in appropriate biomes. To obtain a bee, break a hive with a net. You will take damage. The hive drops a queen and worker bees. Workers come in four types: 1. Workers — pollinate crops and produce honey and royal jelly. 2. Healers — heal sick bees. 3. Builders — breed offspring. 4. Guards/Attackers — protect the hive or attack others. Royal jelly is required to heal bees. Each bee consumes honey. Bees can fight or help each other — sharing honey, royal jelly, or healing. To pollinate, use mod-specific crops planted on stakes. Bees may mutate after 5–10 minutes of real time. There are 19 genes for mutations. Use frames to improve bee living conditions. Honey and royal jelly can be collected, but require 10 collection operations between each. Larvae also require royal jelly to grow. Bees can die of old age. The queen dies when the last drone dies. In addition to producing honey and royal jelly, bees can produce crop products depending on pollination rate per tick. The more they pollinate, the higher the chance to mutate into specific types. For example, a bee might be 30% gold, 20% diamond, 10% emerald, 70% iron, or 30% iridium. This works for all mod crops except the bee’s main flower. Use a bee analyzer for more details.
iu.guide_quest_description.crop: Each plant must be planted on a base block using a trellis. All plants can only be planted on trellises. A plant may turn into weeds. Pollution (radioactive, soil, and air) affects the growth rate of the plant. Growth can be accelerated using bees or fertilizers from the mod. Over time, a plant can mutate — there are 18 genes for modification. Mutation may lead to higher yield, resistance to diseases, or faster growth. However, negative mutations are also possible. Plants can crossbreed if placed next to each other. Crossbreeding results in a hybrid that inherits genes from both parents. Some rare crops can only be obtained through this method. You can use the plant analyzer to check the genes, health, and mutation rate. Some crops require specific conditions like light level, temperature, or soil type. There are three main types: regular crops, agro-cultures, and multi-cultures. Multi-cultures are combinations of several crops in one and produce multiple types of yield. They are harder to maintain but more efficient in the long run. Avoid over-polluting the area or the plants may die.
iu.guide_quest_description.gasvein: The mod features underground veins of gaseous substances: bromine, chlorine, iodine, and fluorine. They generate between Y-levels 5 and 70 and can contain between 40,000 and 240,000 mB of fluid. To detect them, use a gas sensor. When standing in a gas flow, always wear a protective suit — otherwise, you'll suffer from gas poisoning effects.
iu.guide_quest_description.mineralvein: Using a drilling rig, you can detect various types of underground veins. Their appearance depends on the biome: • In mountainous regions, mineral veins are the most common. • In hot biomes — oil veins. • In cold biomes — gas veins (natural gas). To check for a vein, explore each chunk. Mineral veins are extracted using an analyzer with a "Quarry" module and analyzer chests on the sides. You can also use Simply Quarries’ quarries, the Quantum Quarry, or its wireless version (which can mine up to 12 veins simultaneously). To extract oil, use a pump jack. It can be upgraded with a "Productivity" module and configured for wireless resource transfer. Gas veins (natural gas) are extracted using a gas extractor, which can also be upgraded and made wireless. Special gases — fluorine, chlorine, bromine, and iodine — require a gas well. Use it to scan chunks; the chance of discovering a vein is 25%.
iu.guide_quest_description.rubber_tree: Rubber tree generation has been drastically changed. There are now 4 types: regular, two swamp variants, and tropical. As their names suggest, they generate in appropriate biomes. From left to right, their quality improves: • Regular rubber trees have few latex spots that regenerate slowly. • Swamp variants have more spots and faster regeneration. • Tropical ones have the most latex spots and the fastest recovery. Latex is extracted using a tree tap — use it to twist the latex spots on the trunk. Raw latex is obtained first, which must then be processed into liquid latex, and finally dried to get usable latex.
iu.guide_quest_description.other_feature: Special components essential for progression in the mod. They spawn in all water bodies, replacing clay. These are key resources for chemical processes and fertilizers.
iu.guide_quest_description.oil_vein: The mod also includes oil generation. There are 6 types of oil: ranging from light to heavy and from sweet to sour. Each type yields different products when processed. Oil veins only generate in desert biomes.
iu.guide_quest_description.diamond: Break the stones of the crystal vein to get a chance of obtaining diamond ore.
iu.guide_quest_description.smelterystart: Create the components of the smeltery — the required number of each is indicated on the items themselves. Place the main block — the controller — and assemble the structure. As a final step, right-click the controller. Fill the fuel tanks with lava or basalt lava. Basalt lava provides a 1.5x speed boost. Insert ingots, blocks, or ores into the smeltery furnace, wait, and the ingredients will melt. More detailed information on how the smeltery works is shown in the top-left corner of the controller interface.
iu.guide_quest_description.smelteryforms: Required components for casting are placed into the casting block. If you don't need a recipe, it is recommended to remove the mold!
iu.guide_quest_description.electrum: Mix gold and silver in the smeltery.
iu.guide_quest_description.raw_latex: Obtained from rubber trees using a tree tap.
iu.guide_quest_description.steam: Right-click the heater with a water fluid cell.
iu.guide_quest_description.superheated_s: Right-click the heater with a steam fluid cell.
iu.guide_quest_description.ferromanganes: Mix manganese and iron in the smeltery.
iu.guide_quest_description.flint_dust: Put flint into the macerator and grind it.
iu.guide_quest_description.primal_inform: In this age, some machines have a skill system that increases their operation speed by 50% to 400%. In the anvil, a player has a chance to perform the recipe a second time based on skill level. To heat certain machines, place lava underneath them. To repair machines, hold Shift and right-click. These machines have no GUI — all actions must be performed manually. Use right-click to pour or drain fluids.
iu.guide_quest_description.steam_machine: Steam machines consume steam only from steam boilers (regular and multiblock), steam converters, and the peat steam generator. No other sources are valid. Steam can only be transferred by placing machines next to a source or using steam pipes. Superheated steam cannot be transferred via steam pipes — use fluid pipes or fluid cell instead. Lava must be placed under machines to provide heat. Machines require both regular steam and correct pressure to operate. If the pressure is too high or low, they will not work.
iu.guide_quest_description.steamboiler: Operates every 3 ticks, consumes 2 mB of water, and produces 2 mB of steam.
iu.guide_quest_description.steampressure: Requires lava heating underneath. Use "+" and "-" buttons to adjust pressure from 0 to 4. If pressure is not needed, lower it to 0.
iu.guide_quest_description.steam_ampere_: Generates electricity from steam. Requires lava underneath.
iu.guide_quest_description.steam_electro: Requires electricity and lava underneath to operate. Use fluid cells or fluid pipes to extract fluids.
iu.guide_quest_description.steam_convert: Operates every 2 ticks, consumes 1 mB of water, and produces 2 mB of steam.
iu.guide_quest_description.steam_peat_ge: Operates every tick, consumes 1 mB of water, and produces 2 mB of steam.
iu.guide_quest_description.steam_boiler_: Operates every tick, consumes 1 mB of water, and produces 2 mB of steam. Requires heating from a heating machine and the heat exchanger must contain 1 ferrite ring inductor and 10 coils.
iu.guide_quest_description.steam_storage: Transfers steam only from the side it was placed from; sends steam out through other sides.
iu.guide_quest_description.steam_fluid_h: Converts regular steam into superheated steam. Uses steam intended for steam machines.

iu.guide_quest_description.villager:
The mod includes 6 types of villagers: mechanic, engineer, nuclear scientist, metallurgist, chemist, and botanist.

- Mechanic: requires a generator. Sells components for machines and tools that interact with them.
- Engineer: requires a primitive programmable table. Sells components for circuits and sensors.
- Chemist: requires a primitive fluid integrator. Sells chemical components.
- Botanist: requires an apiary. Sells seeds, bees, and items for agriculture and beekeeping.
- Nuclear Scientist: requires an enricher. Sells components related to radioactivity and reactors.
- Metallurgist: requires an anvil from this mod. Sells ingots, plates, and alloys.
iu.guide_quest_description.pipette: Used to empty tanks inside machines. Hover over a tank in the interface and right-click. To clear the pipette, press SHIFT + right-click.
iu.guide_quest_description.recipe_schedu: Allows crafting only specific items inside machines by filtering inputs using a whitelist or blacklist. It is configured via the mechanical recipe scheduler.
iu.guide_quest_description.reactor_simul: Allows you to build reactor schemes under ideal conditions. Inside, you can choose the reactor type and variant. After choosing, an inventory becomes available — place components inside and start the simulation. After starting, reactor performance metrics can be viewed (center-left [i]). You can also write the scheme into a reactor schematic template — simply insert it inside, and the data will be saved.
iu.guide_quest_description.reactor_logic:
The reactor system is deeply simulated. Each component affects the stability of the scheme. There are 3 stability levels:

- **Stable**: components operate without overheating.
- **Unstable**: requires extra cooling, otherwise an explosion will occur after the timer expires.
- **Highly Unstable**: immediate shutdown is recommended, 100% explosion chance.

Reactors have levels that can be upgraded with energy. If a component's level is higher than the reactor’s level, it cannot be inserted.

Fuel rods do not deplete but emit radiation and heat. The goal is to minimize heat. Radiation can be removed via a **protective dome**, and heat via components:
- **Plate** — blocks heat, but increases heat generation by 5–20% per cell.
- **Heat Exchanger** — absorbs lots of heat but does not cool itself.
- **Heat Vent** — cools slightly and absorbs heat.
- **Component Heat Vent** — cools adjacent components but can break from overheating.
- **Capacitor** — absorbs heat, does not cool.
- **Coolant Rod** — passive cooler, needs refilling.
- **Energy Coupler** — connects rods, increases energy output by 50–65% but also increases heat by 0–20%.
- **Neutron Protector** — similar to energy connector, more reliable and reduces heat generation by 5–20%.
iu.guide_quest_description.space:
Space is a vast and fascinating part of the gameplay in this mod. However, it’s not as straightforward as it may seem. Here, you won’t simply fly to planets, mine ores manually, or fight bosses. Space is represented as a simulation where the player takes specific steps to explore it. Each celestial object has two parameters: lens level and exploration percentage.

What is the lens level? To explore, you need a special lens, ranging from level 1 to 7, crafted as you progress through the mod. Each lens level unlocks new planets and moons, along with new fluids and ores.

To start exploration, you send expeditions. Each expedition increases exploration percentage by 5%. Thus, it takes 20 successful expeditions to reach 100% exploration. There are four types of spacecraft: rover, probe, satellite, and rocket. Each comes in four variants — from basic to advanced.

The information panel for each object shows which spacecraft are available. Place the desired craft on the launch pad and press the "Send" button. You can also view which resources are available to mine, but they unlock progressively with exploration percentage. Some resources can only be collected by specific crafts.

At the bottom left is an info panel showing all the key parameters for a successful expedition: flight duration, required fuel and its level, spacecraft level, and necessary modules.
iu.guide_quest_description.colony:
Each colony has a level and accumulates experience produced by workers. As the level increases, new buildings for upgrades become available. The available buildings depend on the colony’s current level. To add a building, hold it in your hand and click the slot at the top right.

A shortage of workers causes problems with food and productivity. One worker in a factory produces 2 units of food. Mines allow resource extraction, and fluid plants handle liquids. Starting from level 7, the "worker happiness" indicator becomes available. The lower the happiness, the lower the efficiency.

To gather resources and fluids, you need to build a warehouse. To send resources, press the "Send" button. Resource transfer requires no fuel or energy. You can also automate the process with a special button.

It’s crucial to create energy and oxygen generators! If problems arise, you’ll have only 30 seconds to fix them. Otherwise, the colony will be destroyed. Defense is mandatory: without it, the colony will deteriorate. All current issues appear in the bottom left of the interface. You can also view the warehouse and the list of resources and fluids available for extraction at the current level.

iu.guide_quest_description.fluid_item_pi: The mod features its own pipes, whose mechanics differ from those in other mods. To operate, you need two pipes: an output pipe and an input pipe. The minimum working distance is 2 blocks, meaning the output pipe should be placed next to the block from which resources or fluids will be extracted, and the input pipe should be placed next to the block where they will be delivered. Each side has its own filters — whitelist and blacklist. Hold an item or an item containing a fluid to configure the filter. To check whether the filter is active, configure it from the side of the block that will be connected to the pipe. You can also set it to respond to a redstone signal. Pipes transfer every 2 ticks, moving the maximum possible amount of items or fluid.
iu.guide_quest_description.fluid_reactor: The liquid reactor accepts water and coolant. The liquid reactor produces steam and hot coolant. The reactor can be accelerated by pressing the up arrow in the interface. Energy is required to accelerate the reactor. Each reactor component has a level. It can be placed inside if the reactor’s acceleration level is equal to or higher than its level. Pressure increases reactor performance but also increases consumption. Each safety level operates differently. Yellow level — you have 15 minutes before an explosion. Red level — you have 2.5 minutes before an explosion. A full radiation indicator causes an explosion. Liquids can be filled through liquid inputs. Liquids can be extracted through liquid outputs.
iu.ferromanganese.hammer.info: §7On the surface of the vein, there are rocks indicating that ores are located beneath them. You need to break these rocks with this §ehammer §7to have a certain chance of obtaining ore. To find out the drop rates and which ores can be obtained, check the recipe in the §emineral separator §7for this mineral.
iu.pressure_steam.info: §7Place it directly next to machines — there are no pipes for transferring §epressure§7. It only transfers §epressure §7when in direct contact, and set it  exactly to the maximum §epressure §7value of the connected machine.
iu.guide_quest_description.gas_reactor: The gas-cooled reactor accepts helium and water. The gas-cooled reactor produces hydrogen and oxygen. The reactor can be accelerated by pressing the up arrow in the interface. Energy is required to accelerate the reactor. Each reactor component has a level. It can be placed inside if the reactor’s acceleration level is equal to or higher than its level. Place the "Pump" item into the recirculation pump. Place the "Fan" item into the intercooler. Adjust the pressure in the compressor. Coolants consume reactor energy for cooling. Helium is slightly recovered in the regenerator if energy is available. Each safety level operates differently. Yellow level — you have 15 minutes before an explosion. Red level — you have 2.5 minutes before an explosion. A full radiation indicator causes an explosion. The reactor can be turned on with the square-shaped button. Liquids can be filled or extracted through tanks. Heat sinks (non-component) must not be placed in the reactor.
iu.guide_quest_description.graphite_reac: The graphite-water reactor accepts carbon dioxide and water. The graphite-water reactor produces steam and oxygen. The reactor can be accelerated by pressing the up arrow in the interface. Energy is required to accelerate the reactor. Each reactor component has a level. It can be placed inside if the reactor’s acceleration level is equal to or higher than its level. Place the "Radiator" item into the heat exchanger. Place the "Condenser" item into the condenser. Adjust the horizontal slot indices for the heat exchanger and condenser. The heat exchanger lowers temperature in the given vertical slot (place it where the rods are). The condenser lowers the temperature generated by the rods. Place graphite rods in the control monitor. The monitor shows the horizontal slot index. Submerging a rod increases the reactor temperature and its consumption but also increases energy generation. Absence of graphite rods leads to reactor overheating. Each safety level operates differently. Yellow level — you have 15 minutes before an explosion. Red level — you have 2.5 minutes before an explosion. A full radiation indicator causes an explosion. The reactor can be turned on with the square-shaped button. Liquids can be filled or extracted through tanks. Heat exchangers and condensers must not be placed in the reactor.
iu.guide_quest_description.heat_reactor: The high-temperature reactor accepts helium and water. The high-temperature reactor produces oxygen and hydrogen. The reactor can be accelerated by pressing the up arrow in the interface. Energy is required to accelerate the reactor. Each reactor component has a level. It can be placed inside if the reactor’s acceleration level is equal to or higher than its level. Place the "Pump" item into the circulation pump. Place the "Electric Motor" item into the pump. Adjust the horizontal slot indices for the graphite control monitor. The pump and electric motor reduce temperature. Place graphite rods in the control monitor. Submerging a rod increases the reactor temperature and its consumption but also increases energy generation. Absence of graphite rods leads to reactor overheating. Each safety level operates differently. Yellow level — you have 15 minutes before an explosion. Red level — you have 2.5 minutes before an explosion. A full radiation indicator causes an explosion. The reactor can be turned on with the square-shaped button. Liquids can be filled or extracted through tanks.

intro.faq.general.independent.question: Why did the mod become independent from IC2?
intro.faq.general.independent.answer: Industrial Upgrade needed more technical freedom to expand its own systems. IC2 could not provide that freedom because of both technical limits and licensing. Because of this, the mod continues in the spirit of industrial technology mods, but without a direct dependency on IC2, similar in idea to how GregTech developed its own direction.
intro.faq.general.frequent_updates.quest: Why is the mod updated so often?
intro.faq.general.frequent_updates.answe: Updates are released often to fix specific issues and add new mechanics without forcing players to wait for a large accumulated patch. The system may feel unusual, but the main goal is to make the mod more comfortable and stable for players as quickly as possible.
intro.faq.general.size.question: Why is the mod so large? Is it a GregTech copy?
intro.faq.general.size.answer: The mod is large because it is constantly expanding and contains many interconnected mechanics. It may look similar to GregTech in scale because GregTech is also a very large technology mod, but Industrial Upgrade follows its own systems and design goals.
intro.faq.general.unusual_mechanics.ques: Why do some mechanics feel unusual?
intro.faq.general.unusual_mechanics.answ: Some mechanics feel unusual because they are implemented differently from what players usually see in other mods. This uniqueness is one of the main differences between Industrial Upgrade and many other technology mods.
intro.faq.general.addons.question: Why expand the main mod instead of making everything as add-ons?
intro.faq.general.addons.answer: The main goal is to create a deep and polished technology mod where players can develop in different ways and choose their own path toward progress. This approach is inspired by long-form progression modpacks such as SevTech.
intro.faq.general.auto_addons.question: Why are some add-ons downloaded automatically or made required?
intro.faq.general.auto_addons.answer: Some add-ons are downloaded automatically to simplify progression for players who are not familiar with optional module management. Other add-ons became required because they improve progression balance and integration with the main mod.
intro.faq.general.ideas.question: Where can I suggest ideas?
intro.faq.general.ideas.answer: Use the Discord server. The link is available on the third page and in the guide. Sometimes polls about future mechanics are also held there.

intro.page3.title: PAGE 3
intro.markdown.default:
![Industrial Upgrade is a large technology mod that expands Minecraft survival from early tools and simple automation to large factories, complex infrastructure, space colonies, genetics, energy networks, and advanced production chains. The mod increases the depth and challenge of progression while keeping the experience focused on planning, experimentation, and long-term development.](https://i.postimg.cc/BvjBhjXS/preview-01.png)

![Industrial Upgrade has its own ore generation system. Ores are generated in veins, and each vein has a defined composition and spawn chances. On first login, the player receives an Ore Scanner tablet that helps locate nearby ore deposits.](https://i.postimg.cc/y6mXr4YZ/preview-02.png)

![The mod progression is divided into several eras:
- Primitive
- Steam
- Electric
- Basic](https://i.postimg.cc/RhdtCmNp/preview-03.png)

![Further progression continues through:
- Improved
- Advanced
- Perfect
- Photonic](https://i.postimg.cc/7Pn0n3m1/preview-04.png)

![Industrial Upgrade also includes an in-game guide that helps players progress through the mod step by step.](https://i.postimg.cc/J47J6s2f/preview-05.png)

![The mod contains many different energy branches:
- Coal industry
- Redstone industry
- Oil industry
- Gasoline
- Diesel](https://i.postimg.cc/XqsZSgzT/preview-06.png)

![Additional power systems include steam turbines, steam generators, reactor technologies, solar panels, wind power, water power, thermal power, and gas power. Reactor gameplay includes liquid, gas, graphite, and high-temperature reactors.](https://i.postimg.cc/KvYk7dKF/preview-07.png)

![Space is implemented as an exploration and colony-management system rather than a classic rocket-only dimension system. Players can establish colonies, develop their population, and use them to extract ores and fluids.](https://i.postimg.cc/ncws307m/preview-08.png)

![Space map](https://i.postimg.cc/wMDkznp7/preview-09.png)

![Planet information](https://i.postimg.cc/VLcRgsqM/preview-10.png)

![Colony system](https://i.postimg.cc/vZ0tGWpL/preview-11.png)

![The mod adds more than 80 crops that can interact with each other and with the mod's bees. This makes it possible to obtain resources through agriculture. Crops can be improved by crossbreeding, mutation, and genetic technologies.](https://i.postimg.cc/3wWCNkhM/preview-12.png)

![Crops and bees](https://i.postimg.cc/2yN40TZm/preview-13.png)

![Industrial Upgrade adds five bee types tied to different biomes. Their mechanics are very different from vanilla bees. Bees are divided into gatherers, builders, healers, and defenders. They can mutate, accelerate crop growth, and eventually produce additional loot besides honey and royal jelly.](https://i.postimg.cc/9f4ZVsPC/preview-14.png)

![The mod includes many armor sets, from basic armor to energy-powered nano-spectral equipment. Players can use jetpacks, charge tools through an energy backpack, and protect themselves from volcanic gases and radiation.](https://i.postimg.cc/DZLG4sYN/preview-15.png)

![Credits and license:
Source code: AGPLv3
Textures and assets: CC BY-NC-ND
Required and core add-ons:
Power Utilities — energy conversion
Simply Quarry — automated quarries
Quantum Generators — quantum energy generation
You are free to use this mod in modpacks.
Please share feedback, bug reports, crashes, and dupe reports on GitHub or Discord.
Industrial Upgrade is positioned as a spiritual successor to IndustrialCraft², but it is not affiliated with the IC2 development team.](https://i.postimg.cc/L6HPhJky/preview-16.png)

iu.guide_quest_description.anvil: A manual workstation for early metal processing. It lets the player forge simple parts and blanks before electric machines are available.
iu.guide_quest_description.forge_hammer: A hand tool for forging. It is used to turn materials into plates, casings or simple blanks during early metalworking.
iu.guide_quest_description.casings: A casing blank for machines. It forms the strong body that later holds working parts, slots and energy components.
iu.guide_quest_description.squeezer: Squeezes raw and plant materials, separating liquid or soft fractions from them. It is useful for latex, organics and early chemistry.
iu.guide_quest_description.dryer: Dries wet materials and prepares them for further processing. It removes excess liquid when normal smelting or crafting is not enough.
iu.guide_quest_description.primal_heater: A simple fluid heater. It works with an internal tank and brings fluids to a hot state for early processing chains.
iu.guide_quest_description.molot: A heavy manual hammer for rough processing of strong materials. It is used when a basic hammer is no longer enough.
iu.guide_quest_description.compressor: Compresses materials into dense forms such as plates, blocks and pressed blanks. It is used where pressure matters more than heat.
iu.guide_quest_description.primal_rollin: Rolls metal into sheets and thin blanks. It is used for plates, casings and parts that need a flat shape.
iu.guide_quest_description.primal_wire_i: Applies insulation to conductors. It turns bare wire into safe cable for power networks.
iu.guide_quest_description.macerator: Crushes ores, stone and materials into dust or crushed blanks. It prepares material for washing, smelting, chemistry and separation.
iu.guide_quest_description.silicon_handl: Processes silicon material and grows a usable crystal. This is a separate stage before precise silicon processing for electronics.
iu.guide_quest_description.primal_fluid_: Soaks an item with fluid from its tank. It is used when a solid blank must absorb a solution or chemical reagent.
iu.guide_quest_description.silicon_cryst: A silicon blank for electronics. After precise processing it becomes a base for boards and advanced circuits.
iu.guide_quest_description.primal_laser_: Polishes crystals and precise parts with a laser. It is used when a clean surface is needed without rough mechanical damage.
iu.guide_quest_description.steam_polishe: A steam-powered polishing machine. It processes crystals and parts without electric power.
iu.guide_quest_description.oxygen: Oxygen is an oxidizer and reagent used by electrolyzers, gas combiners, fluid integrators and separators. It takes part in producing oxides, acids and some products from ore or space materials.
iu.guide_quest_description.primal_gas_ch: Runs reactions with gases inside a work chamber. It handles gas reagents and items when fluid machines are not enough.
iu.guide_quest_description.sulfurtrioxid: Sulfur trioxide is produced in the gas combiner from sulfur oxide and oxygen. It is then mixed with water to make sulfuric acid and is used in sulfur chemistry.
iu.guide_quest_description.fluidcoppersu: Copper sulfate solution is a copper-bearing liquid reagent. It is used in fluid integrators and mixers for copper, circuit board and chemical material processing.
iu.guide_quest_description.circuit_board: A blank board for electronics. Traces and components are added to it before it becomes a working circuit.
iu.guide_quest_description.primal_progra: A table for programming simple boards. It writes control logic into a prepared board and turns it into an electronic component.
iu.guide_quest_description.programmed_ci: A circuit board with stored control logic. It is used as a ready control part in machines and electronic assemblies.
iu.guide_quest_description.steam_handler: A steam machine for heavy ore processing. It separates dense ore material without requiring an electric network.
iu.guide_quest_description.impurity_coal: Coal dust with impurities. It is an intermediate material that must be cleaned or mixed into a usable carbon blend.
iu.guide_quest_description.alloy_coal_du: A prepared carbon blend for metallurgy. It provides controlled carbon content for steel and alloy work.
iu.guide_quest_description.steel: A strong structural metal for casings, tools and machines. It supports mechanisms that are too demanding for early metals.
iu.guide_quest_description.steel_hammer: A durable hammer for harder materials. It lasts longer than early hammers and suits steel blanks.
iu.guide_quest_description.block_strong_: A reinforced anvil for heavy manual processing. It is made for strong alloys and operations that exceed a basic anvil.
iu.guide_quest_description.refractory_fu: Melts ores and materials into molten metals. It handles high-temperature processing and outputs liquid metal for casting.
iu.guide_quest_description.mini_smeltery: A compact casting unit. It accepts molten metal and casts it into basic solid blanks.
iu.guide_quest_description.primal_solder: A soldering mechanism for early electronics. It joins boards, contacts and small parts into finished electronic components.
iu.guide_quest_description.steam_pump: A steam-powered pump for moving fluids. It moves water, lava and other fluids without an electric motor.
iu.guide_quest_description.steam_quarry: A steam quarry. It consumes steam and drill pipes while mining blocks from its working area downward.
iu.guide_quest_description.adv_steam_qua: An improved steam quarry. It keeps the pipe-and-steam mining logic while working faster and more conveniently.
iu.guide_quest_description.titanium_stee: A strong alloy for high-load parts. It resists heat and wear better than ordinary steel.
iu.guide_quest_description.electronics_a: Assembles electronic components from boards, conductors and small parts. It is useful for repeated circuit and module production.
iu.guide_quest_description.electronic_ci: A basic electronic circuit. It provides simple control logic for electric machines and upgrades.
iu.guide_quest_description.machine_casin: A machine body that holds working slots, energy and mechanical parts. It is the base of most mechanisms.
iu.guide_quest_description.blast_furnace: The main block of the blast furnace. It controls multiblock smelting for materials that require high temperature.
iu.guide_quest_description.elemotor: An electric motor for machines with moving parts. It turns electric energy into mechanical motion.
iu.guide_quest_description.liqued_heater: An electric fluid heater. It heats tank contents for processes where the fluid temperature matters.
iu.guide_quest_description.generator: Burns solid fuel and produces EF. It is a simple power source for early electric networks.
iu.guide_quest_description.redstone_gene: Produces EF from redstone materials. It provides compact power from redstone without liquid fuel.
iu.guide_quest_description.geogenerator: Produces EF from hot fluids, mainly lava. It is the power source for the geothermal line.
iu.guide_quest_description.solid_refrige: Creates cooling power from snowballs, snow, ice, packed ice and blue ice. The item is consumed, fills the cooling buffer for a set time, and the stored cooling is then supplied to machine cooling systems.
iu.guide_quest_description.base_machines: The core set of electric processors: crushing, compressing, extracting, smelting, rolling, cutting and extruding. They form the base of automated resource processing.
iu.guide_quest_description.generator_mic: A control chip for generators. It acts as the electronic controller for power machines.
iu.guide_quest_description.alloy_smelter: Smelts several materials together into alloys. It handles recipes that a normal furnace cannot mix correctly.
iu.guide_quest_description.gearing: Manufactures gears and toothed parts. These parts are used by machines that transfer motion mechanically.
iu.guide_quest_description.electronic_as: Automatically assembles electronic parts from boards, wires and components. It speeds up advanced electronics production.
iu.guide_quest_description.plastic_creat: Creates plastic from prepared chemical components. It is the chemical machine for the polymer chain.
iu.guide_quest_description.plastic_plate: Forms plastic into plates. It makes flat plastic blanks for casings, insulation and parts.
iu.guide_quest_description.welding: Welds metal parts into strong assemblies. It is used where ordinary joining is not strong enough.
iu.guide_quest_description.orewashing: Washes crushed ore and separates useful material from waste rock. It improves ore quality before further processing.
iu.guide_quest_description.nitrate_dust: A nitrate powder for chemical reactions. It acts as a solid reagent in nitrogen chemistry and material treatment.
iu.guide_quest_description.item_divider: Splits items into smaller recipe-defined parts. It is useful for fractions, dusts and intermediate materials.
iu.guide_quest_description.nitrogen: Nitrogen is used for chemistry and cooling. It is part of nitrogen-hydrogen compounds, nitrogen oxides and acids, is accepted by the fluid cooling machine, and appears in space/material recipes.
iu.guide_quest_description.advanced_circ: An advanced circuit with more complex control logic. It is used by machines that need more than a basic board.
iu.guide_quest_description.gas_sensor: A portable gas and pollution sensor. It shows unsafe atmosphere and helps monitor the area around the player.
iu.guide_quest_description.gas: Natural gas is extracted from gas deposits and used as both fuel and chemical feedstock. Sensors and wells locate it, pumps move it, and turbines or chemical machines consume it.
iu.guide_quest_description.triple_solid_: Mixes three solid components into one material. It is used for powders and blends with strict proportions.
iu.guide_quest_description.gas_combiner: Combines gases into new compounds. It works as a gas reactor for nitrogen, hydrogen and other chemistry.
iu.guide_quest_description.nitrogenhydri: Nitrogen hydride is produced from nitrogen and hydrogen. It is an intermediate gas for later nitrogen reactions and some space processing recipes.
iu.guide_quest_description.nitrogenoxy: Nitrogen oxide is an intermediate in nitrogen chemistry. The gas combiner oxidizes it into nitrogen dioxide, leading into nitric acid production.
iu.guide_quest_description.nitrogendioxi: Nitrogen dioxide is the active gas used to make nitric acid. In the gas combiner it mixes with water to produce acid for etching, plastics and chemical processing.
iu.guide_quest_description.nitricacid: Nitric acid is a strong liquid reagent. It is used by gas combiners, fluid integrators, plastic processing, refractory processing and some space-rock recipes.
iu.guide_quest_description.fluid_integra: Injects a fluid into an item or material. It is used for soaking, dissolving and chemical treatment of solid blanks.
iu.guide_quest_description.silver_nitrat: Solid silver nitrate. It is a reagent for special processing and silver-related chemistry.
iu.guide_quest_description.crushed_urani: Crushed uranium ore. It belongs to radioactive processing and needs dedicated handling instead of the normal ore line.
iu.guide_quest_description.radioactive_h: Processes radioactive ores separately from ordinary machines. It prepares uranium material for purification and nuclear work.
iu.guide_quest_description.industrial_or: Deeply purifies ore material after crushing and washing. It removes excess impurities and outputs cleaner material.
iu.guide_quest_description.se_sensor: A solar energy sensor. It is used by solar equipment to read light and generation conditions.
iu.guide_quest_description.se_gen: A solar generator that works from light. It produces energy without fuel while light conditions are valid.
iu.guide_quest_description.gen_sunnarium: Creates sunnarium plates for solar technology. It prepares the material in a form suitable for panels and solar parts.
Creates sunnarium elements and panels. It processes solar-energy materials for generator assembly.
iu.guide_quest_description.calcium_carbi: Calcium carbide, a solid reagent for acetylene production. It is used in the polymer chemistry chain.
iu.guide_quest_description.acetylene: Acetylene is a flammable hydrocarbon gas. Chemical machines combine it with hydrogen into ethylene, and it is also used by gas turbines and organic/plastic chains.
iu.guide_quest_description.polymerizer: Runs polymerization of fluids and gases. It turns chemical feedstock into polymer materials.
iu.guide_quest_description.solid_fluid_i: Combines a solid component with a fluid in one process. It is used when an item must bind or absorb a reagent.
iu.guide_quest_description.polyeth: Polyethylene is a liquid polymer material. It is used in plastic production and polymerizer chains as a base for plastic parts and insulation materials.
iu.guide_quest_description.fluid_separat: Separates a fluid into multiple components. It is useful for mixtures, fuels and petrochemical intermediates.
iu.guide_quest_description.propane: Propane is a flammable gas and petrochemical reagent. It is used by gas turbines, gas combiners and organic processing where a light hydrocarbon is needed.
iu.guide_quest_description.bromine: Bromine is a chemical reagent and gas resource. It is connected to gas wells/sensors, electrolyzers, gas combiners and recipes involving space materials.
iu.guide_quest_description.propylene: Propylene is an organic gas for polymer processing. It is used by polymerizers and fluid integrators to produce plastics or organic intermediates.
iu.guide_quest_description.plast: Finished plastic for insulation, casings and machine parts. It is the solid form of polymer material.
iu.guide_quest_description.plastic_plate: A plastic plate for casings, insulation and machine assembly. It is the flat form of finished plastic.
iu.guide_quest_description.handler_ho: Processes heavy ores and minerals, separating useful material from waste. It handles resources that normal machines process poorly.
iu.guide_quest_description.refiner: Refines oil and heavy fluids into useful fractions. It is the central machine of fuel and petrochemical processing.
iu.guide_quest_description.gen_disel: A diesel generator. It produces EF from dense liquid fuel from the oil chain.
iu.guide_quest_description.gen_pet: A petrol or light-fuel generator. It uses liquid fuel for stable EF production.
iu.guide_quest_description.fluid_cooling: Creates cooling power for machine cooling networks by consuming liquid nitrogen, hydrogen or helium from its tank. Hydrogen fills cooling faster, nitrogen works slower, and helium is the most economical option; the stored cooling is provided through CoolComponent to connected machines.
iu.guide_quest_description.simple_wind_g: A wind generator. It produces EF from wind, depends on placement conditions and consumes no fuel.
iu.guide_quest_description.simple_water_: A water generator. It gets energy from water and suits constant generation when installed correctly.
iu.guide_quest_description.adv_alloy_sme: An improved alloy smelter. It processes complex metal mixtures faster and more conveniently.
iu.guide_quest_description.centrifuge: Separates mixtures by spinning. It works with dusts, fluids and materials that must be split into fractions.
iu.guide_quest_description.enrichment: Enriches raw material by increasing the useful fraction. It is used before advanced ore and radioactive processing.
iu.guide_quest_description.nuclear_waste: Recycles nuclear waste and recovers useful components. It helps reduce dangerous leftover buildup.
iu.guide_quest_description.radioactive_w: A dangerous residue from nuclear processing. It needs dedicated storage and special machines, not normal processing.

iu.guide_quest_description.motoroil: Motor oil is a lubricant fluid for machine processes. It participates in processing recipes where the machine needs technical lubrication.

iu.guide_quest_description.rover_assembl: Assembles rovers from a body, wheels, modules and electronics. The machine creates the final rover item with parameters used later on planets.
iu.guide_quest_description.probe_assembl: Assembles research probes from a body, electronics and modules. Probes are used to study celestial bodies and obtain data or resources in the space system.
iu.guide_quest_description.satellite_ass: Assembles satellites from a body, electronics and modules. Satellites act as space equipment for research and resource collection from orbit or celestial bodies.
iu.guide_quest_description.rocket_assemb: Assembles a rocket from a body, engine, tanks and modules. The result stores rocket parameters and is used by the launch pad for flight preparation.
iu.guide_quest_description.hydrazine: Hydrazine is the basic rocket fuel. It is accepted by the launch pad, fluid mixer and rover/rocket fuel level system.

iu.storage_system.tooltip.consumption.de: Consumed by this mechanism while it is connected to an active storage network.
iu.guide_quest_description.storage_syste: The mod adds a storage system for items and fluids. The system requires a controller, which converts EF into the system's own energy and keeps it running. Storage cell blocks, the cells themselves, and a terminal are required to store resources. Blocks can be connected directly to each other or through cables. Every system block, except the controller, creates a connection node, so cables are not required between adjacent blocks. The system can store up to 300 unique item types and up to 50 fluid types. Autocrafting requires a pattern terminal and a pattern. There are two pattern modes: crafting table and machine. Crafting table mode is used for regular crafting table recipes, while machine mode is used for recipes of selected machines. The maximum number of inputs and outputs is 36. To place an item or block into a recipe, hold it in your hand and click the slot. To add a fluid, hold a capsule or any fluid container that contains the required fluid and click the slot. Use the middle mouse button to switch the slot mode and switch it back. Use the mouse wheel to change the amount. To change the amount faster, use Shift, Ctrl, or both keys together. When the recipe is configured, press the arrow under the pattern. Crafting table patterns are placed into the crafting table interface, while machine patterns are placed into the machine interface. Autocrafting also requires a processor. Each processor tier can run 2^(n - 1) processes and does not require a separate memory amount. For example, a basic processor can run 1 craft regardless of its size. Use the fluid terminal to view fluids, and the autocrafting terminal to view autocrafting tasks. To import and export resources externally, use the corresponding item and fluid buses. Each bus type has its own modes: blacklist and whitelist for import buses, resource insertion mode for export buses — sequential or random — and redstone signal control for all buses.
iu.guide_quest_name.space_worlds: Flight to Planets, Moons, and Asteroids
iu.guide_quest_description.space_worlds: After researching an object to 100%, you can travel to different space objects, but the flight depends on several conditions. First, you need to craft a Planetary Translocator. Charge it and open its interface. In the interface, select a system and the desired planet. To open a planet's moons, click the planet. After that, press the teleport button. Several required conditions must be met before teleportation. The main requirement is electric armor from the mod, from nano armor to spectral armor. An oxygen module for the armor is also required. There are additional requirements as well. During object research, rovers may have required heat or cold modules. The thermal protection module is the armor equivalent of that requirement: the same number of modules shown in the research table must be installed in the armor. If the world has pressure, a pressure module is required. Teleportation consumes energy depending on your distance from Earth. The counter in the lower-left corner shows the required information. If you accidentally drop the required item, you will be teleported back to Earth. If the charge runs out, you will also be teleported back to Earth. Asteroids contain different ores.
